executiveSummary.text: Fed holds at 3.5-3.75% with highest dissent since 1992 (4 votes), signaling rates higher-for-longer amid energy-driven inflation. Powell staying on as governor indefinitely.
Semiconductor index SOX surged +35% in April — best month since the 1970s. Demand broadening from pure AI hyperscalers to enterprise/edge. Intel's best day since 1987.
Gold holding ~$4,640-4,660 amid oil-driven inflation. Real rates rising (nominal up, inflation expectations potentially faster) creates near-term headwind. Bank of America maintains $6,000 target.
Oil remains elevated (~$102) driven by US-Iran war. ExxonMobil CEO: 'market hasn't seen the full impact.' Energy inflation could force Fed to reconsider rate path.
Singapore STI started May with a strong open (+1%). Singapore banks (DBS, OCBC, UOB) benefit from S$ strength and stable NIM. Energy sector under pressure from oil shock.

regime.label: Stagflationary Expansion — Energy Shocks + AI Infrastructure Buildout Coexist

usGlobal.what: Semiconductor index surged +35% in April — historic tape
usGlobal.why: Renewed confidence in AI buildout revenue materializing; demand broadening from hyperscalers to enterprise. Intel's Xeon 6 and AI accelerator wins drove best day since 1987.
usGlobal.implication: AI Infra tranche (420k SGD = 16%) benefits directly. KLAC, AMAT, LRCX all in equipment space — earnings revision cycle likely moving higher. NVDA at $5T market cap post-earnings. Hold overweight on semis.

usGlobal.what: Fed holds at 3.5-3.75%, 4 dissentions — most since 1992
usGlobal.why: Inflation elevated (statement explicitly cited energy prices). Markets now pricing zero cuts for rest of 2026. Powell staying on as governor post-term, creating uncertainty around future FOMC direction.
usGlobal.implication: Higher-for-longer is headwind for long-duration assets. REITs (Ascendas, CICT, MLT, FCT) under pressure from elevated discount rates. Foundation tranche (40.4% = ~1.06M SGD) partially protected by floating rate exposures (OCBC, UOB). Duration risk in VWRA/QQQ minor — short-term.

usGlobal.what: Gold holds $4,640-4,660; oil pulls back to $101.94 from highs
usGlobal.why: Gold caught between oil-driven inflation (should be bullish) and rising real rates (should be bearish). Oil pullback (-3%) on Iran deal speculation or demand destruction signals. BofA $6,000 target stands.
usGlobal.implication: Metals tranche (175k SGD = 7%) — gold position neutral. Oil shock 1st order: inflation up, Fed can't cut. 2nd order: real rates rise, dollar firms. 3rd order: gold (zero yield, real-rate sensitive) gets pressured. This is NOT a 'crisis = gold up' scenario. Long-term thesis intact but near-term turbulence.

singapore.what: Singapore STI started May strong (+1%), banks supported by S$ firmness
singapore.why: S$ likely supported by USD/SGD trading near 1.34-1.35. Singapore's energy import costs rising (oil shock) but domestic financial sector benefits from regional risk-on. DBS, OCBC, UOB are yielding attractively vs. peers.
singapore.implication: SG Div tranche (442k SGD = 17%) — bank dividend yields 4-5%+. REITs under pressure from higher rates. Singapore energy importers (Keppel, Sembcorp) face margin compression from oil. Infrastructure names (Sembcorp, Keppel) mixed — power demand from AI data centers a long-term tailwind.

singapore.what: US-Iran war affecting credit scores, Spirit Airlines shutdown — 1st war-linked bankruptcy
singapore.why: Geopolitical supply shock creating demand destruction in travel sector. Aviation fuel costs spiking. Oil supply disruption tightening energy markets globally.
singapore.implication: EWY (Korea ETF, 8.4% of portfolio via Asia allocation) — exposure to energy-importing Asian economies with current account pressures. Not directly in holdings but monitors.

commodities.what: Gold resilient but vulnerable — oil-driven inflation the key variable
commodities.why: TD Securities: gold at mercy of oil prices. Oil shock = inflation up = Fed can't cut = real rates rise = gold headwind (not tailwind). The crisis-gold narrative is a 1st-order shortcut.
commodities.implication: Gold position (metals tranche) — hold. Long-term scarcity thesis intact. Near-term: track oil/Iran developments closely. If oil spikes above $110 and stays, real rates surge — gold could retest $4,400. If Iran ceasefire, oil pulls back to $85-90, gold rallies to $4,800+.

commodities.what: Uranium — no fresh catalyst in past 48hrs, but multi-year structural bull intact
commodities.why: Nuclear renaissance driven by AI power demands and data center nuclear PPA deals. Kazatomprom output constraints persist. GRID (Global X Uranium ETF) held in portfolio.
commodities.implication: GRID position — hold. Uranium structural thesis unchanged: AI data centers need 24/7 firm power, nuclear is the only scalable zero-carbon firm power. Kazatomprom supply deficit (~20% of global conv. uranium) won't resolve in 2026.

narratives.title: AI Revenue Is Real — Semis Broadening Beyond Hyperscalers
narratives.description: April 2026 semiconductor rally was not just NVDA. AMAT, KLAC, LRCX, AMD, MU all participating. AI chip industry revenue forecast at $1.1T in 2026. Tape described as 'historic' — winning streaks not seen since 1970s.
narratives.priceImpact: strongly positive for AI Infra tranche

narratives.title: Energy Shock Transmitted Via Inflation — Fed Trapped
narratives.description: US-Iran war driving oil above $100. Fed statement explicitly cited energy prices. April FOMC had 4 dissentions — highest since 1992. Powell staying on as governor (not just chair) suggests institutional continuity but also uncertainty about leadership transition.
narratives.priceImpact: negative for duration bonds, mixed for gold

narratives.title: Google's Best Month Since 2004 — AI Revenue Accelerating
narratives.description: Alphabet added ~$1.2 trillion in market value in April alone. GOOG in KL's portfolio as a proxy for AI application layer. This validates the thesis that AI spending is converting to revenue across the ecosystem, not just infra.
narratives.priceImpact: positive for AI application layer names

narratives.title: Semiconductor Equipment — The Picks-and-Shovels of AI
narratives.description: ASML, AMAT, KLAC, LRCX all hitting highs. Equipment companies benefit from EVERY AI buildout regardless of which chip designer wins. Morningstar raises KLAC price target to $1,500. AMAT earnings revision cycle positive.

secondOrderEffects.text: Oil shock → inflation up → Fed can't cut → real rates rise → dollar firms → gold gets crushed vs. cash (NOT 'crisis = gold up'). Gold is a REAL RATE hedge, not a geopolitical hedge. In inflationary shocks with trapped central banks, gold underperforms until real rates peak.
secondOrderEffects.implication: Monitor real 10Y yield closely. If 10Y real yield breaks above +2%, gold faces material headwind. Current metals allocation (7%) appropriate; do NOT add aggressively until oil/Iran situation resolves.

secondOrderEffects.text: Semiconductor rally broadening → equipment makers' earnings revisions going up → capital expenditure cycle extending → copper/electrical infrastructure demand sustained → copper miners (COPX) and utilities benefit at 2nd order.
secondOrderEffects.implication: Copper (GRIN/FCX) and energy infrastructure names benefit from AI buildout 2nd order. Grid modernization, electrical infrastructure = long-duration tailwind. Watch ELEC/construction plays as portfolio extension.

secondOrderEffects.text: Fed trapped at 3.5-3.75% with 4 dissentions → split FOMC signals higher volatility in rate expectations → USD potentially stronger near-term (dollar carry) → SGD may strengthen slightly → Singapore importers (energy) face margin pressure; local consumers benefit from S$ purchasing power.
secondOrderEffects.implication: Singapore bank NIM stable-to-improving. REITs under pressure (rate sensitivity). Keppel/Sembcorp energy margin compressed by oil. Singapore economy: stagflationary (oil cost push + rate stability). Growth resilient but inflation above MAS comfort zone.

secondOrderEffects.text: Google +$1.2T in April → AI application revenue confirming → hyperscaler capex remains elevated → TSMC/ASML/AMAT all benefit → AI infrastructure buildout cycle extended from 2-3 years to 4-5 years.
secondOrderEffects.implication: TSM and ASML in AI Infra tranche are structural holds. ASML down one EUV system in Q1 (reported earlier) but order book remains full. Intel's Xeon 6 success is a validation of general-purpose compute + AI hybrid workloads.

investment.thesis: Add to semiconductor equipment exposure via KLAC, AMAT, LRCX on any pullback. AI capex cycle extending — equipment makers benefit from ALL AI chip production regardless of designer winner. Morningstar raised KLAC to Buy with $1,500 target.
investment.trigger: Any SOX pullback of 5-8% from current levels. KLAC at or below $900.
investment.whyNow: April's +35% SOX move may pause, creating entry. AI infrastructure buildout still in early innings. Equipment order backlog 18-24 months.
investment.invalidator: Hyperscaler capex cuts >20% in Q2 earnings. ASML EUV shipment halt (export controls escalating). AI demand recession.

investment.thesis: Hold gold position. BofA $6,000 target intact. Oil-driven inflation is near-term headwind for gold but scarcity thesis (debt monetization, central bank buying, USD reserve erosion) remains structurally bullish.
investment.trigger: Gold retesting $4,400-$4,500 support zone on oil spike. Iran ceasefire news bringing oil below $90.
investment.whyNow: Portfolio needs inflation hedge that is NOT oil (oil is already a position). Gold vs. oil ratio near multi-year lows — historically a mean-reversion opportunity.
investment.invalidator: Fed cuts rates aggressively (contradicted by current split FOMC). Dollar loses reserve status faster than expected (structural tail risk, not base case).

trading.thesis: AVGO vs. NVDA relative trade: AVGO outperforming post-earnings as 'efficiency winner.' AMD also outperforming as cheaper AI GPU alternative. If AVGO breaks above $1,400, add to AI Infra tranche.
trading.trigger: AVGO earnings confirmation of AI networking/silicon switching demand. AMD MI300X adoption hitting critical mass.
trading.whyNow: AVGO is the 'picks-and-shovels' of AI efficiency — every inference call needs networking. Underowned vs. NVDA in retail portfolios.
trading.invalidator: Custom silicon (Google TPUs, Amazon Inferentia) displacing merchant silicon. AI infrastructure spending slowdown.

trading.thesis: Short WTI crude if Iran ceasefire confirmed — oil likely to correct 15-20% from current $102. Currently elevated on geopolitical risk premium.
trading.trigger: Iran-US diplomatic talks resuming. Strait of Hormuz NOT closed. China demand destruction from trade war.
trading.whyNow: Oil at $101 is pricing in worst-case Iran disruption. Any de-escalation brings immediate $10-15/barrel downside. ExxonMobil CEO commentary suggests market hasn't priced full impact yet — suggesting upside risk before reversal.
trading.invalidator: Iran strikes on Gulf infrastructure. US military action against Iran. Strait of Hormuz actual closure.

watchlist.thesis: Singapore REITs (Ascendas, CICT, MLT, FCT) — under pressure from higher rates but AI data center demand creating long-term re-rating opportunity. Monitor if 10Y yield stabilizes below 4.5%.
watchlist.trigger: Fed signaling rate cut timeline (currently none priced). Singapore inflation moderating. AI data center land acquisitions in Singapore announced.
watchlist.whyNow: REITs yields 5-6% but capital values declining with discount rate. Long-term structural demand from AI infrastructure (Singapore as APAC hub) could re-rate sector.
watchlist.invalidator: Rates stay higher-for-longer. Singapore property cooling measures expanded. Office vacancy rates rising.

watchlist.thesis: MU (Micron) — memory cycle turning. AI servers need HBM memory, enterprise SSD demand. Cheaper valuation vs. compute peers.
watchlist.trigger: MU earnings (next report). HBM pricing stabilization. AI server memory content per box increasing 40%+.
watchlist.whyNow: MU down ~30% from 2025 highs despite AI infrastructure buildout benefiting memory. Memory is the most cyclically depressed segment of semis — mean reversion candidate.
watchlist.invalidator: Memory oversupply persisting into 2027. SK Hynix/Samsung capacity not rationalizing. AI server memory content not growing as fast as expected.

riskWatch.name: Oil Spikes Further — Iran Escalation
riskWatch.description: ExxonMobil CEO expects market hasn't seen full Iran war impact. If oil breaks above $110 sustained, inflation psychology breaks upward. Fed forced to consider rate HIKES (not just holds). Real rates surge, gold and REITs get crushed, credit spreads widen.
riskWatch.mitigation: Portfolio has limited direct oil exposure. Short WTI crude trade idea ready. Gold position as partial inflation hedge. REITs are the vulnerable point — rate sensitivity highest.

riskWatch.name: Fed Credibility / Leadership Uncertainty
riskWatch.description: Powell staying on as governor (not just chair) creates uncertainty. 4 dissentions at April meeting = FOMC is not unified. Markets need clear Fed signaling; split committee creates policy ambiguity. USD could be more volatile.
riskWatch.mitigation: SGD-denominated portfolio partially insulated from direct USD rate volatility. Singapore MAS likely on hold for 2026. DBS/OCBC/UOB NIM stable.

riskWatch.name: Semiconductor Valuation — AI Hype vs. Reality
riskWatch.description: SOX +35% in one month = very stretched near-term. P/E multiples re-rated aggressively. Any earnings miss (NVDA, AMAT, ASML) could cause sharp correction. The 'broadening demand' narrative needs earnings confirmation.
riskWatch.mitigation: AI Infra tranche already has significant unrealized gains. Take some profit on AMAT if above $180. KLAC raised to Buy — position size already appropriate. Do NOT add aggressively at current levels.

keyEvents.text: FOMC holds rates at 3.5-3.75% — 4 dissentions, highest since 1992
Powell to stay on as Fed governor post-term, awaiting investigation closure
SOX +35.2% in April — 'historic' semiconductor tape since 1970s
Intel best day since 1987 after earnings/guidance beat
Google adds $1.2T market cap in April — best month since 2004
WTI crude $101.94; ExxonMobil CEO sees higher prices ahead
ECB holds rates, cites Iran conflict inflation pressure
Singapore STI starts May +1%; banks support
Berkshire cash surges under Abel's first quarter as CEO

whatMatters: Oil above $100 + Iran war = energy inflation re-accelerating. Fed trapped at 3.5-3.75%, not cutting. Real rates rising — this is bearish gold near-term despite the 'crisis' narrative.
whatToDo: Hold AI Infra (semis, equipment, energy). April's semiconductor rally broadening beyond hyperscalers — validates thesis. SOX +35% = take some profits if AMAT/KLAC re-test highs, but don't exit.
Hold gold. Long-term $6,000+ thesis intact. Near-term turbulence from real rates is not a structural break. Portfolio allocation (7%) is appropriate.
Monitor oil/Iran developments daily. Short WTI on Iran ceasefire is the highest-probability near-term trade. If ceasefireconfirmed, oil -$15/barrel within days.
Singapore banks (DBS, OCBC, UOB) — hold. NIM stable in higher-for-longer environment. REITs — underweight until rate clarity.
whatToIgnore: 'Crisis = gold up' heuristic. Gold is a real-rate asset. In inflationary shocks with trapped central banks, gold underperforms. The Kitco gold article confirms this nuance — not a simple safe-haven bid.
Day-to-day chip stock volatility without earnings changes. April's +35% SOX move is momentum, not thesis change. Don't rotate into/out of AI Infra on monthly performance numbers.
Short-term FX noise. USD/SGD at 1.34-1.35 is stable. Singapore's current account remains healthy despite energy import costs.
